div1.entryFree:
Fire, vb. (dissyll. in H6C II, 1, 83 and Caes. III, 2, 260). 1) to set on fire, to kindle: another wind that --s the torch. Lucr. 315. he (the sun) --s the proud tops of the eastern pines, R2 III, 2, 42. hasty powder --d, Rom. V, 1, 64. f. the traitors' houses, Caes. III, 2, 260. a beacon --d, Per. I, 4, 87. Tropically, == to inflame: that --s all my breast, H6C II, 1, 83. a heart new --d, Caes. II, 1, 332. might f. the blood of ordinary men, III, 1, 37.
2) to drive by fire: till my bad angel f. my good one out, Sonn. 144, 14 (cf. Cor. IV, 7, 54 and Caes. III, 1, 171). f. us hence like foxes, Lr. V, 3, 23.
3) to discharge: that lead which is --d from a gun, LLL III, 63. let all the battlements their ordnance f. Hml. V, 2, 281.
